paper_title: Triangle-free subgraphs with large fractional chromatic number
summary: Conjecture 4 from arXiv:1808.01605 asks whether for every real x≥1 and integer g≥3 there exists k(x,g) such that every graph G with χ_f(G)≥k(x,g) contains a subgraph H of girth at least g with χ_f(H)≥x. The source paper itself establishes the g=4 case (Theorem 3), which is the fractional analog of Rödl's 1977 result on chromatic number. No subsequent paper resolving the conjecture for g≥5 was found across targeted web searches; the conjecture appears to remain open as of 2026.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is about extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs; not related to Conjecture 4 of Mohar-Wu.
Paper is about Hadwiger's conjecture for 4- and 5-chromatic graphs; not related to fractional chromatic number and girth.
Paper is about clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph (Hadwiger-type); not related to Conjecture 4.

Paper resolves the fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio problem (Dvořák–Ossona de Mendez–Wu conjecture). WebFetch confirmed the abstract concerns Hall ratio, not girth constraints; no mention of Mohar-Wu Conjecture 4.

search_queries: fractional chromatic number subgraph girth Mohar Wu conjecture 1808.01605
fractional chromatic number large girth subgraph high girth 2024 2025 arxiv
notes: The conjecture is the natural full generalization of the paper's main Theorem 3 (g=4 case) to all girth g≥3, paralleling the classical Erdős-Hajnal / Rödl program for ordinary chromatic number. None of the five supplied internal references address this conjecture. No follow-up paper proving or disproving the full statement for g≥5 was found; the conjecture is 8 years old, so medium rather than high confidence is appropriate.
